skill.name: gitea
skill.description: Gitea operations via tea CLI. Use when user mentions: gitea, tea, or when git remote shows a Gitea instance.
skill.summary: Gitea operations via tea CLI. Use when user mentions: gitea, tea, or when git remote shows a Gitea i...
skill.author: ETeissonniere

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-style command examples and several weak-cryptography patterns. The shell findings are legitimate external command guidance for the tea and git CLIs, while the weak-cryptography hits are false positives on documentation text such as description and Desc. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Can Modify Gitea State
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md recommends tea and git commands that can create pull requests, merge pull requests, create or close issues, add comments, clone repositories, and fork repositories. This is expected for a Gitea helper, but it can change remote project state if the assistant runs commands without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and include side-effecting tea operations. The context is legitimate documentation, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Authentication and Repository Detection Use Local CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill instructs the assistant to run git remote -v, tea login list, and output-format commands. These are common and low-complexity checks, but they still depend on local command execution and installed credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in SKILL.md and match the static external command findings. They are ordinary Gitea workflow commands with no injection or obfuscation evidence.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at lines 3, 37, and 46, but these lines contain frontmatter text and command placeholders using description or Desc. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain documentation labels and placeholder text, not cryptographic code. No evidence of MD5, DES, RC4, or other weak algorithm use was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Side-Effecting tea Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents tea commands for merging pull requests, closing issues, commenting on issues, creating pull requests, and forking repositories. These commands should require clear user intent before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands clearly perform write operations against Gitea. The documentation does not show malicious intent or hidden execution.

content.user_title: Manage Gitea Workflows with tea CLI
content.value_statement: Teams using Gitea often need consistent command guidance for pull requests, issues, and repository actions. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use the tea CLI with safer workflow checks.
actual_capabilities: Detects likely Gitea repositories by checking git remotes.
Guides authentication checks with tea login list.
Provides tea commands for creating, listing, viewing, checking out, and merging pull requests.
Provides tea commands for creating, listing, viewing, closing, and commenting on issues.
Shows repository clone and fork commands for Gitea projects.
Recommends JSON or YAML output for machine-readable tea results.
limitations: Requires the tea CLI to be installed and configured.
Requires valid Gitea authentication before remote operations.
Does not choose safe merge or issue actions without user intent.
Does not replace repository permissions or branch protection rules.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: List, inspect, check out, and merge Gitea pull requests through documented tea commands.

use_cases.title: Manage Project Issues
use_cases.description: Create, review, close, and comment on issues while keeping work inside the Gitea workflow.

use_cases.title: Automate Repository Checks
use_cases.description: Use machine-readable tea output to support scripted status checks and workflow summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Check Gitea Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this repository appears to use Gitea and verify my tea CLI login status.

prompt_templates.title: List Active Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the open Gitea issues and pull requests for this repository using tea.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my git status, then help me create a Gitea pull request with a clear title and description.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Workflow State
prompt_templates.prompt: Use machine-readable tea output to summarize open issues, active pull requests, and any actions that need confirmation.

output_examples.input: Check whether I can use tea in this repository.
output_examples.output: The repository remote does not point to GitHub or GitLab, so it may be a Gitea instance. The next step is to verify tea authentication before running repository actions.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare a pull request.
output_examples.output: The workflow starts by checking for uncommitted changes. After the branch is ready, the skill can guide a tea pull request command with a title and description.
The skill can list open issues and pull requests, then summarize which items need review, comments, checkout, or explicit user approval.

best_practices: Confirm the target repository and Gitea account before write operations.
Check git status before creating or updating pull requests.
Use machine-readable output when summarizing issues or pull requests.
anti_patterns: Do not merge pull requests without explicit user approval.
Do not close issues based only on a brief summary.
Do not assume a non-GitHub remote is always Gitea.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides Gitea pull request, issue, and repository operations through the tea CLI.

faq.question: Does it require Gitea credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The tea CLI must be authenticated before protected Gitea operations can run.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the tea command pattern for creating pull requests with a title and description.

faq.question: Can it manage issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports command guidance for creating, listing, viewing, closing, and commenting on issues.

faq.question: Does it support structured output?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends JSON or YAML output for workflows that need machine-readable results.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it guides external CLI commands that can modify Gitea resources.
